channel.title: AI Press Review
channel.itunes:author: David Perron
channel.itunes:subtitle: Factual, Clear, Built for Busy Professionals
channel.itunes:summary: 40 sources. 15 minutes. What matters in AI today.
channel.description: Every weekday morning at 7AM CET, AI Press Review distills 40+ global sources into a focused 15-minute briefing — filtered, weighted, and structured around six pillars: AI News, Research & Breakthroughs, Use Cases, Tools & Practice, Weak Signals, and Education. Built for professionals who need signal, not noise. Every Saturday: a weekly recap of the five trends that defined the week.

item.title: OpenAI's "Spud" Model & Compute Crunch — Apr 14
item.description: Today's review highlights OpenAI's upcoming "Spud" model, promising significant product improvements, amidst a growing industry-wide compute shortage impacting AI development and deployment. We also cover new agentic AI tools from Google and AWS, and discuss the critical need for efficient LLM inference strategies as demand surges.
item.content:encoded: Today's review highlights OpenAI's upcoming "Spud" model, promising significant product improvements, amidst a growing industry-wide compute shortage impacting AI development and deployment. We also cover new agentic AI tools from Google and AWS, and discuss the critical need for efficient LLM inference strategies as demand surges.
item.itunes:summary: Today's review highlights OpenAI's upcoming "Spud" model, promising significant product improvements, amidst a growing industry-wide compute shortage impacting AI development and deployment. We also cover new agentic AI tools from Google and AWS, and discuss the critical need for efficient LLM inference strategies as demand surges.

item.title: Japan's AI Alliance: Can Industry Beat Giants? — Apr 13
item.description: Japan's top industrial and financial firms are uniting with SoftBank to build a sovereign AI foundation, reducing reliance on US and Chinese models. Meanwhile, AI adoption in healthcare, finance, and manufacturing is accelerating, with measurable gains in efficiency and decision speed — though deployment gaps and security risks persist.
item.content:encoded: Japan's top industrial and financial firms are uniting with SoftBank to build a sovereign AI foundation, reducing reliance on US and Chinese models. Meanwhile, AI adoption in healthcare, finance, and manufacturing is accelerating, with measurable gains in efficiency and decision speed — though deployment gaps and security risks persist.
item.itunes:summary: Japan's top industrial and financial firms are uniting with SoftBank to build a sovereign AI foundation, reducing reliance on US and Chinese models. Meanwhile, AI adoption in healthcare, finance, and manufacturing is accelerating, with measurable gains in efficiency and decision speed — though deployment gaps and security risks persist.

item.title: Gemma 4 vs. Agent Limits: On-Device Power Meets Real-World Gaps — Apr 13
item.description: Google's Gemma 4 brings powerful, private agentic AI to phones with full on-device processing, while new research reveals that agent skills often fail under real conditions. Meanwhile, startups and labs are pushing open models, edge deployment, and smarter tool use—highlighting both the promise and pitfalls of today’s AI agents.
item.content:encoded: Google's Gemma 4 brings powerful, private agentic AI to phones with full on-device processing, while new research reveals that agent skills often fail under real conditions. Meanwhile, startups and labs are pushing open models, edge deployment, and smarter tool use—highlighting both the promise and pitfalls of today’s AI agents.
item.itunes:summary: Google's Gemma 4 brings powerful, private agentic AI to phones with full on-device processing, while new research reveals that agent skills often fail under real conditions. Meanwhile, startups and labs are pushing open models, edge deployment, and smarter tool use—highlighting both the promise and pitfalls of today’s AI agents.
